tier_justification: Tier 0 exemption. Predicate checked against the real diff, not a self-assigned label: (a) SINGLE WORK-UNIT — one file, skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md, two paragraphs deleted; (b) NO OUTWARD OR IRREVERSIBLE SURFACE — `gh pr diff 273 --name-only` returns no path under hooks/ or scripts/, no .sh/.ts/.js/.py file, no UI, CLI output, rendered artifact or served endpoint, so rule 6's user-facing predicate does not fire and no publish/deploy/migration/data-deletion/external-send is involved; (c) GOAL STATE DIRECTLY COVERED — the goal is the absence of specific prose, which a grep over merged state asserts exactly. Note the exemption is still a written artifact with executable evals carrying negative controls, not an absence.

evals.assert: Row 1 of the token-burn measures is absent from skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md — no surviving reference to the deleted rule by any of its four naming forms.
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails && git show origin/main:skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -qiE 'row 1 of 4|single largest saving|MANDATORY compaction|Compaction cadence'; test $? -eq 1
evals.negative_control: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails && git show 51154b33c9f0f7222734cf59c6f541da0f082703~1:skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -qiE 'row 1 of 4|single largest saving|MANDATORY compaction|Compaction cadence'; test $? -eq 1
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (pattern absent post-merge). negative_control exits 1 (pattern present at the pre-deletion parent commit), proving the check discriminates rather than always passing.

evals.assert: The three surviving token-burn measures carry coherent, self-consistent numbering — no rule label references a denominator that counts the deleted rule, and no incoherent label (e.g. 'row 4 of 3') exists.
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails && test $(git show origin/main:skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -coE 'row [123] of 3|rows [123] and [123] of 3') -ge 3 && ! git show origin/main:skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -qE 'of 4|row [4-9] of 3'
evals.negative_control: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails && test $(git show 51154b33c9f0f7222734cf59c6f541da0f082703:skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -coE 'row [123] of 3|rows [123] and [123] of 3') -ge 3 && ! git show 51154b33c9f0f7222734cf59c6f541da0f082703:skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -qE 'of 4|row [4-9] of 3'
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (three coherent 'of 3' labels, zero stale 'of 4' denominators). negative_control exits 1 against the pre-renumber commit 51154b3, where the labels still read 'of 4' — proving the check detects the stale-denominator defect rather than passing unconditionally.

evals.assert: No dangling reference: the deletion leaves no surviving prose anywhere in the repo that references row 1 or its enforcement gate as if it still exists.
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails && git grep -iE 'row 1 of 4|rows 1 and 4|rows 1-4|als_gate_compaction_per_stop' origin/main -- ':!*.next/*' ':!node_modules/*' > /tmp/e3.out 2>&1; test ! -s /tmp/e3.out
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (no dangling row-1 references on merged main). negative_control exits 1 (a term that IS present produces output), proving the empty-output assertion detects real content rather than always reporting empty.

evals.assert: Semantic collateral avoided: the Context-window persistence section's opening guidance ('Do not stop work early because the context window is filling') is SEPARATE general guidance and must survive the deletion.
evals.cmd: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails && git show origin/main:skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -q 'Do not stop work early because the context window is filling'
evals.negative_control: cd /Users/harrison/Github/coderails && git show origin/main:skills/agentic-loop/SKILL.md | grep -q 'Do not stop work early because the banana is filling'
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (the surviving guidance is present). negative_control exits 1 (a near-identical but false string is absent), proving the grep matches the specific sentence rather than any nearby text.

amendments.why: Oracle went stale, assertion did not. E2 was frozen at 51154b3, when the surviving rules still read 'row 2/3/4 of 4'. PR review then found a real defect that freeze: deleting row 1 left the survivors declaring a denominator of 4 while only 3 rules remained, so the skill counted a rule that no longer existed. The fix (commit e44b55a) renumbered them to rows 1/2/3 of 3, which correctly broke E2's literal grep for 'row N of 4' — verified live by the fixing worker, exit 1 against HEAD. E2's underlying claim (surviving rules carry coherent numbering) was always the point and is unchanged; only the pattern encoding it was rewritten, and strengthened to also assert the absence of any stale 'of 4' denominator or incoherent 'row 4 of 3' label. The negative control now runs against 51154b3 itself, so it fires on exactly the defect review caught. No grader verdict existed at amendment time, so no re-grade is owed under rule 5.
